PAST HIGHER EXAM SOLUTIONS 89 JUNE 2011 90 JUNE 2012 98 JUNE 2013 103 JUNE 2014 106 JUNE 2015 111 Table of Integrals 114

The 2017 to 2019 exam papers will be provided on Moodle.

PAST EXAM PAPERS

Please note that the emphasis of the exam may change from year to year and that it is not possible to test all aspects of the course in a 2-hour examination. Hence, these papers are only a guide as to the style and level of difficulty of our first year examinations. The exam format may be different in 2020 вҖ” check Moodle for details. The solutions to the examination papers contained here have been written by many members of staff of the School of Mathematics and Statistics. While every care is taken to excluded errors, we cannot guarantee that the solutions are error-free. Please report any serious errors to the Director of First Year Mathematics. Exam papers from 2019 will be provided on Moodle for practice before the exam. Students are encouraged to produce their own solutions to the 2019 exam papers and discuss these on Moodle.

MATH1131 November 2010 1. i) Let z = 1 + i. a) Find |z|. b) Find Arg(z). c) Use the polar form of z to evaluate z 28 and then express your answer in Cartesian form. ii) Suppose that (x + iy)(3 + 2i) = (4 + 7i) where x, y вҲҲ R. Find the value of x and the value of y. iii) a) Find all solutions to the equation z 6 = 1, where z вҲҲ C. b) Hence, or otherwise, express z 6 вҲ’ 1 as a product of three quadratic polynomials with real coefficients. iv) Let the region S be defined as

S = {z вҲҲ C : |z + i| = 1}.

Sketch the region S on a carefully labelled Argand diagram. v) Consider the following MAPLE session.

> A:=<<1,0,-sqrt(3)>|<0,2,0>|<sqrt(3),0,1>>; пЈ® вҲҡ пЈ№ 1 0 3 пЈҜ пЈә A := пЈҜ 0 2 0 пЈә вҲҡ пЈ° пЈ» вҲ’ 3 0 1 > A^2; пЈ® вҲҡ пЈ№ вҲ’2 0 2 3 пЈҜ пЈә пЈҜ 0 4 0 пЈә вҲҡ пЈ° пЈ» вҲ’2 3 0 вҲ’2 > A^4; пЈ® вҲҡ пЈ№ вҲ’8 0 вҲ’8 3 пЈҜ пЈә пЈҜ 0 16 0 пЈә вҲҡ пЈ° пЈ» 8 3 0 вҲ’8 > A^6; 64 0 0 пЈҜ 0 64 0 пЈә пЈ° пЈ» 0 0 64

Use the above Maple session to find the inverse of the matrix A2 .

vi) A car dealer sells three brands of cars: Audis, BMWs and Chevrolets. Her costs consist of registration, GST and the manufacturerвҖҷs price of the car. She has to pay registration costs of $200 per Audi, $600 per BMW and $400 per Chevrolet. The GST charges are $1, 800 per Audi, $2, 400 per BMW and $2, 000 per Chevrolet. To the manufacturer she pays $20, 000 per Audi, $30, 000 per BMW and $26, 000 per Chevrolet. Last year her total registration bill was $12, 000, the total GST tax bill was $65, 600 and she paid a total of $784,000 to manufacturers. Let a, b and c denote the number of Audis, BMWs and Chevrolets she sold last year. a) Write down a system of linear equations in a, b and c determined by the above information. b) Convert the system of equations to an augmented matrix form and hence find a, b and c by performing Gaussian elimination on the augmented matrix.   1 0 1 2. i) Let P = . 2 3 0 a) Evaluate the matrix product P P T . b) State the size of the matrix P T P . ii) Let A, B and C be points in R3 with position vectors пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 1 3 a = пЈӯ2пЈё , b = пЈӯ0пЈё and c = пЈӯ4пЈё respectively. 3 1 7 вҲ’вҲ’ вҶ’ вҲ’вҶ’ a) Find AB and AC. b) Find a parametric vector equation of the plane that passes through the points A, B and C. iii) Determine the coordinates of the point of intersection of the line пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ x 1 1 пЈӯy пЈё = пЈӯ2пЈё + t пЈӯвҲ’1пЈё for tвҲҲR z 5 1

and the plane x вҲ’ 3y + z = 15. пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 вҲ’2 пЈ¬3пЈ· пЈ· and w = пЈ¬ вҲ’6 пЈ· be two vectors in R4 . пЈ¬ пЈ· iv) Let v = пЈ¬ пЈӯвҲ’1пЈё пЈӯ ОІ пЈё 5 вҲ’10 Find the value of ОІ so that the vectors v and w are perpendicular. пЈ« пЈ¶ v) Let u = пЈӯ1пЈё be a vector in R3 . a) Find the magnitude of u. b) Write down a vector with a magnitude of 10 which is parallel to u.

vi) Find the determinant of the matrix пЈ«пЈ¶ 3 1 0 C = пЈӯ4 1 7пЈё . 1 2 0

vii) Suppose that u, v and w are distinct non-zero vectors in Rn with the property that

projw (u) = projw (v).

Prove that (u вҲ’ v) is perpendicular to w.

3. i) Evaluate the limit

x2 ex lim . xвҶ’0 1 вҲ’ cos(ПҖx)

ii) Determine all real values of a and b such that the function f given by ( x2 for x вүӨ 1, f (x) = 2 вҲ’x + ax + b for x > 1,

is differentiable at x = 1. iii) Let L = lim . xвҶ’вҲһ x2 + 1

a) Evaluate L. b) Given any Оө > 0, find a constant M (which may depend on Оө) such that we have

вҲ’L < Оө x2 + 1

whenever x > M .

вҲҡ iv) Let f (x) = x3 + 3 x вҲ’ 5 for all real x. a) Use the Intermediate Value Theorem to prove that f has at least one positive real root. b) By considering f вҖІ , or otherwise, show that f has only one real root. dy v) Use logarithmic differentiation to calculate for y = (sin x)x . dx vi) A curve in R2 is given in polar coordinates as

r = 6 sin Оё, where 0 вүӨ Оё вүӨ ПҖ/2.

a) Express the equation of the curve using Cartesian coordinates x and y and state the range of x and the range of y. b) Hence, or otherwise, sketch the curve in the xy-plane.

4. i) Evaluate the following integrals:

1вҲ’x Z a) I1 = dx; (1 + x)3 Z ПҖ b) I2 = x cos 2x dx. ii) Determine whether the improper integral Z вҲһ 1 + sin x K= dx 1 3x2

converges or diverges. iii) a) State the definitions of sinh x and cosh x in terms of the exponential function. d b) Hence find an expression for cosh(ax) in terms of sinh(ax), where a is a constant. dx c) Simplify the expression coshвҲ’1 cosh(вҲ’4726) . 

iv) A continuous function f satisfies the equation Z x Z 1 x16 x18 1 f (t) dt = t2 f (t) dt + + вҲ’ . 0 x 8 9 9

By differentiating this equation with respect to x, and using the First Fundamental Theorem of Calculus where needed, find f (x). v) Let g be a function defined by

g(x) = tanвҲ’1 (x) + tanвҲ’1 (1/x).

a) What is the (maximal) domain of g? Give reasons for your answer. b) By examining g вҖІ (x), show that g is piecewise constant on its domain. c) Hence, or otherwise, determine the exact value of

tanвҲ’1 (вҲ’eОұ ) + tanвҲ’1 (вҲ’eвҲ’Оұ ), for all Оұ вҲҲ R.

MATH1131 JUNE 2011 1. i) Let z = вҲ’1 вҲ’ i. a) Find |z|. b) Find Arg(z). c) Use the polar form of z to evaluate z 102 and then express your answer in Cartesian form. ii) a) Simplify (2 + 4i)2 . b) Hence, or otherwise, solve the quadratic equation z 2 вҲ’ 4z + (7 вҲ’ 4i) = 0. iii) Sketch the following region on the Argand diagram ПҖ S = {z вҲҲ C : 0 вүӨ Arg(z вҲ’ i) вүӨ }.

iv) Evaluate the limit lim вҲҡ . xвҶ’вҲһ x вҲ’ x2 вҲ’ 6x вҲ’ 4 v) Evaluate the improper integral Z вҲһ xвҲ’5/4 dx .

vi) A curve in the plane is defined implicitly by the equation

x2 вҲ’ 3xy 2 + 11 = 0 .

a) Show that the curve has slope at the point (x, y) given by

dy 2x вҲ’ 3y 2 = . dx 6xy

b) Find the equation of the tangent to the curve at the point (1, 2). c) Write a Maple command to plot the curve in the region 1 вүӨ x вүӨ 4 and вҲ’5 вүӨ y вүӨ 5.

2. i) Use De MoivreвҖҷs Theorem to prove that

cos(4Оё) = 8 cos4 Оё вҲ’ 8 cos2 Оё + 1.

ii) Consider the line в„“ with parametric vector equation пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ x 1 3 пЈӯy пЈё = пЈӯ2пЈё + пЈӯ2пЈё t, for t вҲҲ R. z 4 5

a) Give two points on the line. b) Give a vector parallel to the line. c) Explain why the line в„“ is perpendicular to the plane P with Cartesian equation

9x + 6y + 15z = 24.

d) Find a point on the line whose y-coordinate is 0. iii) Consider the following Maple session: > A:=<<1,1,0>|<-1,1,0>|<0,0,sqrt(2)>>; 1 вҲ’1 0 A := пЈҜ 1 1 0 пЈә вҲҡ пЈ° пЈ» 0 0 2 > A^2; пЈ® пЈ№ 0 вҲ’2 0 пЈҜ пЈә пЈҜ 2 0 0 пЈә пЈ° пЈ» 0 0 2 > A^4; вҲ’4 0 0 пЈҜ 0 вҲ’4 0 пЈә пЈ° пЈ» 0 0 4 > A^8; 16 0 0 пЈҜ 0 16 0 пЈә пЈ° пЈ» 0 0 16

Use the above MAPLE session to find the inverse of A7 . iv) Evaluate the limit (x вҲ’ 1)2 lim . xвҶ’1 1 + cos(ПҖx)

v) Evaluate the indefinite integral Z x sin(2x) dx.

vi) The function f has domain [0, 1] and is defined by f (x) = ex + ax, where a is a positive constant. a) Prove that 2 is in the range of f . b) Prove that f has an inverse function f вҲ’1 . c) Find the domain of f вҲ’1 .

пЈ« пЈ¶   1 3 1 4 7 3. i) Let A = and B = пЈӯ0 5пЈё. 2 1 3 1 0 a) Find AB. b) What is the size of the matrix BA? ii) A pet shop has x hamsters, y rabbits and z guinea pigs. Each hamster eats 50g of dry food and 40g of fresh vegetables, and needs 1m2 of space. Each rabbit eats 300g dry food and 320g of fresh vegetables, and needs 5m2 of space. Each guinea pig eats 100g of dry food and 200g of fresh vegetables, and needs 3m2 of space. Altogether they eat 2900g of dry food and 3920g of fresh vegetables, and need 63m2 of space. a) Explain why 5x + 30y + 10z = 290. b) Write down a system of linear equations that determine x, y and z. c) Reduce your system to echelon form and solve to find the number of hamsters, rabbits and guinea pigs. iii) The points A, B and C in R3 have position vectors пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 2 4 a = пЈӯ0пЈё , b = пЈӯ3пЈё and c = пЈӯ1пЈё 1 4 5

respectively. Write down a parametric vector equation of the plane passing through A, B and C. iv) Consider the following system of linear equations.

x+yвҲ’z =2 2x + 3y + z = 6

a) Using Gaussian Elimination find the general solution to the system of equations. b) Hence, or otherwise, find a solution to the system with the property that the sum of the x, y and z coordinates of the solution is 0.

пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 3 v) Suppose A, B are two points in R with position vectors 2 and 1пЈё respectively. пЈӯ пЈё пЈӯ 4 5 We let O denote the origin. вҲ’вҶ’ a) Find | OB |. b) Find the area of triangle AOB. c) Hence, or otherwise, find the perpendicular distance from A to the line through O and B. vi) Suppose that u and v are non-zero, non-parallel vectors in R3 of the same magnitude. Prove that u вҲ’ v is perpendicular to u + v.

4. i) a) Give the definition of cosh x. b) Use the definition to prove that

4 cosh3 x = cosh 3x + 3 cosh x .

iii) a) Sketch the curve whose equation in polar coordinates is r = 6 sin 2Оё. dy b) Find the gradient, dx , of this curve at the point where Оё = 16 ПҖ.

iv) A statue 2 metres high stands on a pillar 2.5 metres high. A person, whose eye is 1.5m above the ground, stands at a distance x metres from the base of the pillar. B

A

Оё ПҶ вң· C x 1.5

The diagram shows the above information, with the personвҖҷs eye being at C. a) Prove that d вҲ’1 cotвҲ’1 t =  dt 1 + t2 b) Show that x Оё = cotвҲ’1 вҲ’ cotвҲ’1 x c) Hence find the distance x that maximises the angle Оё.

x2 вҲ’ 5x sin y + y 2 = 4.

Find the equation of the tangent line to this curve at the point (2, 0). vi) Let p(x) = x5 + 5x + 7. a) Explain why p has at least one real root. b) Prove that p has exactly one real root.

2. i) Use De MoivreвҖҷs Theorem to show that

cos(3Оё) = 4 cos3 Оё вҲ’ 3 cos Оё. пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 1 ii) Find the intersection of the line x = пЈӯ 0 пЈё + О» пЈӯ 1 пЈё , О» вҲҲ R, with the plane 5x вҲ’ 2 2 2y + z = 17. iii) Consider the following MAPLE session: > A:=<<0,0,1>|<0,1,0>|<-1,0,0>>; пЈ® пЈ№ 0 0 вҲ’1 пЈҜ пЈә A := пЈҜ пЈ° 0 1 0 пЈ» пЈә

Using the Maple session above, find the inverse of A2001 . iv) The points C and D have position vectors пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 4 c = 2 and d = 1пЈё . пЈӯ пЈё пЈӯ 3 5

a) Find the cross product c Г— d. b) Hence, or otherwise, find the area of the parallelogram with adjacent sides OC and OD, where O is the origin. v) Evaluate the indefinite integral Z x4 ln x dx.

vi) Consider the three functions:

x2 f : R вҶ’ R, f (x) = , 1 + x2 g : (0, 3) вҶ’ R, g(x) = (x вҲ’ 1)2 , вҲҡ h : [1, 5] вҶ’ R, h(x) = 1 + ln x + sin x cos x.

Only one of these functions has a maximum value (on its given domain). Which one is it? Give reasons for your answer. vii) Sketch the polar curve r = 2 вҲ’ 2 cos Оё. You should show any lines of symmetry, and clearly identify where the curve intersects the x and y axes. dy viii) Suppose that y = xsin x . Find . dx

3. i) The points A and B in R3 have position vectors пЈ« пЈ¶ пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 1 a = пЈӯ0пЈё and b = пЈӯ2пЈё . 1 4

a) Find a parametric vector equation of the line l passing through A and B.

b) By evaluating an appropriate пЈ« пЈ¶ dotпЈ«product, пЈ¶ пЈ« show пЈ¶ that the line l from part (a) is x 1 1 perpendicular to the line пЈӯy пЈё = пЈӯ4пЈё + пЈӯвҲ’3пЈё t; t вҲҲ R. z 6 2     1 2 1 1 вҲ’1 1 ii) Let P = and Q = . 3 вҲ’1 4 2 5 0 a) Evaluate P QT . b) What is the size of P T Q? c) Does the matrix product P Q exist? Explain your answer. iii) A system of three equations in three unknowns x, y and z has been reduced to the following echelon form

пЈ« пЈ¶ 1 2 3 5 пЈӯ 0 2 4 8 пЈё. 0 0 Оұ вҲ’9 ОұвҲ’3

a) For which value of Оұ will the system have no solution? b) For which value of Оұ will the system have infinitely many solutions? c) For the value of Оұ determined in part (b), find the general solution. iv) The number of $10, $20, and $50 notes in the cash register at BillвҖҷs Burger Barn is x, y and z respectively. The total value of all the notes in the register is $1020. There are 44 notes in total. Also, the number of $10 notes is equal to the sum of the number of $20 notes and the number of $50 notes. a) Explain why x + 2y + 5z = 102. b) By setting up two further equations and solving the system of three equations in three unknowns x, y and z, determine how many of each type of note is in the cash register.

пЈ¶пЈ« a вҲ’вҶ’ v) The (non-zero) point Q has position vector пЈӯ b пЈё. The vector OQ makes angles Оұ, ОІ c and Оі respectively with the X, Y and Z axes. пЈ« пЈ¶ a) By considering the vector e1 = пЈӯ 0 пЈё show that p a= a2 + b2 + c2 cos Оұ.

b) Deduce that cos2 Оұ + cos2 ОІ + cos2 Оі = 1.

c) If the angles Оұ and ОІ are complementary, that is, their sum is 90в—Ұ , what can be said вҲ’вҶ’ about the vector OQ?

4. i) Find a quadratic function q(x) = x2 + bx + c such that the function ( e3x , if x вүӨ 0 h(x) = q(x), if x > 0

is differentiable at x = 0. ii) Each of the following calculations is expressed in MAPLE. Write each in normal mathematical notation and evaluate. a) arcsin(sin(7*Pi/3)); b) diff(int(exp(t^2),t=0..x^2),x); iii) Prove that lim = 1 as follows: xвҶ’вҲһ x2 + 3 Given any real number З« > 0, find a real number M (expressed in terms of З«), such that if x > M then 2 вҲ’ 1 < З«. x +3 iv) Let f : R вҶ’ R be defined by

f (x) = x3 + sinh x + 1.

a) Explain why f has a differentiable inverse g. b) What is the domain of g? c) Evaluate g вҖІ (1). v) A chemical process produces Factor X, which flows into a 50 litre tank which is initially empty. At time t вүҘ 0, Factor X flows into the tank at the rate of litres per hour. 10 + t2 Will the tank eventually overflow? Explain your answer.
